sourcesUsed: https://www.jobs.ac.uk (keyword search: PhD finance studentship; accountancy-and-finance/business-studies category pages returned unfiltered counts only)
https://www.findaphd.com (category pages still returned HTTP 403 to automated access; not usable this round)
Official university/scholarship pages: Heriot-Watt (hw.ac.uk via jobs.ac.uk advert), UCC, UWE Bristol, University of Limerick, DCU Business School
discoveryNotes: Refresh confirms the Sept-2026 England finance/accounting funded cycle remains closed as of 2026-07-12. Carried forward the two open UWE Bristol Business & Law studentships (compliance/governance fit, deadline 2026-08-16) from batch-wave2-england.json into this agent's primary output. Re-included the Heriot-Watt Edinburgh Business School EPSRC studentship (closes 2026-08-12) per explicit instruction; it was previously filed under skippedWrongRegion in wave2 because Edinburgh is geographically Scotland, but the masters dataset has no separate 'scotland' country slug (England (United Kingdom) is the only UK slug; edinburgh is listed as a UK city under that slug), and the agent's own scope/region is 'UK + Ireland', so it is filed under countrySlug 'england' with a location clarification in notes. Only the Circular Economy Transition (sustainable/climate finance) topic of the two-topic Heriot-Watt advert is included; the AI/labour-economics topic is not a finance/accounting/compliance fit and is excluded. Re-verified UCC CUBS scholarship still open (deadline 2026-08-03). New discoveries this round: University of Limerick Kemmy Business School Doctoral College PhD Scholarship (fully funded, open topic within KBS incl. finance/accounting, deadline 2026-08-31) and DCU Business School PhD/MPhil Tuition Fee-Only Scholarship for part-time students (fees-only, deadline 2026-08-19). DCU trustLAB PhD Scholarship (deadline 2026-08-20, fully funded) was evaluated but excluded — its research focus is organisational/interpersonal trust, not finance/accounting/compliance. No fabricated emails or LinkedIn URLs; all supervisor/contact details are copied verbatim from official pages where named.

phdOffers.title: EPSRC PhD Studentship — Financing the Circular Economy Transition
phdOffers.subject: Finance and investment barriers to circular-economy uptake; financial performance of circular business models, sustainable/transition finance, circular supply-chain investment, climate-related financial risk, ESG and sustainability disclosure, and institutional investors' role in circular innovation and resource security.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 3.5-year EPSRC studentship: UK-fees tuition waiver, enhanced annual stipend £23,985 (10% above standard UKRI rate), plus £3,500/year training and research expenses allowance. Overseas applicants eligible but must cover the UK/overseas fee difference.
phdOffers.notes: Location is Edinburgh, Scotland (UK), not England proper; filed under countrySlug 'england' because the masters dataset uses 'England (United Kingdom)' as the only UK country slug (no separate Scotland entry) and this agent's scope is 'UK + Ireland'. Advert offers 2 studentships across 2 topic areas; only the finance-relevant 'Financing the Circular Economy Transition' topic (select 'Accountancy and Finance PhD' on the application form) is captured here. A second topic, 'AI and Causal Inference for Labour Market Resilience' (Prof. Cristina Tealdi / Dr Yannis Kostas, select 'Economics PhD'), is economics/labour-market focused and out of scope for Dina's finance/accounting/compliance domains. Master's in Finance, Sustainable Finance, Economics, Business Analytics, Data Analytics, Energy Finance or Accounting desirable; Python/R/Stata/Matlab a plus.

phdOffers.institution: UWE Bristol — Bristol Business School (College of Business and Law)
phdOffers.title: PhD Studentship — Water Governance Legal Frameworks and Regional Outcomes
phdOffers.subject: Comparative analysis of water-sector legal and regulatory architecture across England, Wales and Scotland amid privatisation and post-2025 integrated-regulator reforms; ownership, accountability, regional cost/benefit distribution, and climate adaptation governance.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 3-year studentship from 1 Oct 2026: tax-exempt stipend £21,805 p.a. (2025/26 rate cited on page) plus full-time home/international tuition fees for up to three years.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified open on 2026-07-12 (carried forward from batch-wave2-england.json). Reference 2627-OCT-CBL01. Requires 2:1+ and preferably Masters ≥65%; economics/environmental-law cognates accepted. Compliance/regulatory-finance angle, not core accounting/finance.

phdOffers.institution: UWE Bristol — Bristol Business School (College of Business and Law)
phdOffers.title: PhD Studentship — Sexual Harassment and Sexual Assault at Work: Legal and HR Blind Spots
phdOffers.subject: Mixed-methods study of how UK workplace sexual harassment/assault is routed through civil employment law and HR grievance processes rather than criminal justice; policy, compliance, and trauma-informed safeguarding implications for employers and unions.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 3-year studentship from 1 Oct 2026: tax-exempt stipend £21,805 p.a. plus full-time home/international tuition fees for up to three years.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified open on 2026-07-12 (carried forward from batch-wave2-england.json). Reference 2627-OCT-CBL02. Compliance/regulatory-policy angle for Dina; not core finance/accounting. Legal and/or HR qualifications expected.

phdOffers.institution: University College Cork — Cork University Business School (CUBS)
phdOffers.title: CUBS PhD Scholarship 2026 — Management & Marketing (incl. ESG & Green Innovation)
phdOffers.subject: Fully funded PhD scholarship in organisational innovation, innovation strategy, green/ESG innovation, entrepreneurial ecosystems, or related management/marketing topics under Prof. Lawrence Dooley.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: EU tuition fees plus €25,000 annual stipend for 4 years; non-EU fee waiver applied for awardees. Page still shows 'Updated 24 June 2026' as of this verification.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified open on 2026-07-12; no change from prior wave. Research areas include Grand challenges, ESG & Green Innovation (area V). Strong fit for Dina's compliance/ESG angle; broader than pure finance PhD.

phdOffers.institution: University of Limerick — Kemmy Business School (KBS)
phdOffers.title: Doctoral College PhD Scholarship — Kemmy Business School (open topic)
phdOffers.subject: Open-topic scholarship: research proposals welcome in any field aligned with Kemmy Business School faculty expertise (incl. finance, accounting, economics, management), aligned to UL's Research Strategy 2022-2027 and relevant Sustainable Development Goals. Candidate must identify a KBS supervisory team as part of the application.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: EU-level annual postgraduate research fees (currently €5,800) plus €25,000/year stipend for up to 3 years.
phdOffers.notes: No named academic supervisor is pre-assigned; applicant proposes a research topic and must secure a 2-person KBS supervisory team as part of the application (10% of evaluation weight). Administrative submission contact only: Rebecca Gachet, rebecca.gachet@ul.ie (not a supervisor — omitted from supervisors[] to avoid mislabeling). Requires Master's degree or 1st-class Honours Bachelor's; ~3,000-word research proposal. Home to ~70 PhD candidates in KBS. Confidence marked Medium because fit depends on the applicant proposing a finance/accounting-aligned project and finding a willing KBS supervisor, rather than a pre-defined finance project.

phdOffers.institution: Dublin City University — DCU Business School
phdOffers.title: PhD / MPhil Tuition Fee-Only Scholarship for Part-Time Students
phdOffers.subject: Open-topic part-time PhD/MPhil fee scholarship; candidates propose their own research project (incl. finance/accounting) and work under supervision of a DCU Business School academic.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Payment of tuition fees up to the EU fee rate per annum for up to 5 years (part-time PhD) or 3 years (part-time MPhil). Fees only — no stipend or living-cost support.
phdOffers.notes: Open topic, part-time only, fees-only (no stipend) — weaker funding fit than fully-funded offers but explicitly a funded (partial) scholarship rather than self-funded. No named supervisor pre-assigned; applicant proposes topic and secures a DCU Business School supervisor. Confidence marked Medium pending a specific finance-aligned supervisor confirmation.
